behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (git-state + PR-state handoff). PASS2-AUDIT: failure boundary includes real git commit ancestry/time and PR existence from external state; subprocess git/API mismatches are real-infra failures mocks can hide, so upgrade to System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only git/PR state logic ("commits not in main... no open PR"), with no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent sequence, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp repo, branch off main, add backdated commit(s), ensure no PR exists for that branch in the real PR source used by the app, run staleness detection end-to-end, assert stale=true only when age threshold and unmerged/unPR conditions are met.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (bootstrap wiring + detector output). PASS2-AUDIT: session bootstrap behavior depends on real startup path plus real current-branch detection and detector invocation; subprocess/env/repo-state issues are system-boundary failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is warning emission with branch context, not model judgment; no chained agentic decisions, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real session-start entrypoint in a repo prepared to be stale; assert emitted warning appears and includes actionable branch fields (branch name, age, ahead/PR context) from live detector output.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit. PASS2-AUDIT: behavior text explicitly says "local status-classifier function" and input is already "computed branch state"; this is pure mapping logic with no required module/infra boundary. Rejected higher levels: no OS/network/subprocess dependency in this behavior text, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over classifier inputs (clean state, stale-no-pr state, edge flags) asserting exact deterministic label outputs and no side effects.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System (explicit live dependencies). PASS2-AUDIT: behavior text explicitly requires "against live repo and PR state" and rendered command output; mocking can hide real git/API/render integration failures. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call in behavior text and no multi-agent chain; therefore not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in two real scenarios (clean branch; ahead-without-PR stale branch) using live repo + PR source, then assert rendered output classifies each case correctly, including the STALE marker.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (state logic). PASS2-AUDIT: correctness hinges on real PR lifecycle state (open/merged) as represented by the real provider plus git relation; API/state-shape mismatches are real-infra failures, so System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is PR-state filtering only ("open or merged PRs"), with no AI/ML output dependence and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Using real repo/PR backend, create cases for branch with open PR and branch with merged PR, run detector/status command, assert neither is labeled `stale-no-pr`.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System (history-rewrite semantics). PASS2-AUDIT: rebases/squash merges are real git graph transformations typically exercised via git subprocess and repository state; fakes often miss ancestry/merge-base edge cases, so System is required. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is git-history correctness only ("rebases or squash merges"), not AI/ML-dependent and not multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repo, perform branch rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite commit hashes, then run staleness detection and assert branch is not classified `stale-no-pr` once work is represented in main/merged history.
